info.name: Gorgias Public API
info.description: The Gorgias REST API. Please go to https://api.gorgias.com for more details.

response.name: Your account

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

formdata.description: A file to upload. The name of this parameter should be replaced with the label of the file you want to have once uploaded. E.g: `package-damaged.png`.

request.description:
You can upload several files at a time by adding parameters to the root of the request body. The name of each parameter will be used as the label of the file once uploaded. E.g:
  - parameter name: `package-damaged.png`
  - parameter value: Content of the file.

response.name: A list of files

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

formdata.description: A file to upload. The name of this parameter should be replaced with the label of the file you want to have once uploaded. E.g: `package-damaged.png`.

response.name: The created customer.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

query.description: Full name of the customer you're looking for.
The maximum number of objects to fetch per page. Default: 30.
The customer's preferred timezone (format: IANA timezone name).
ID of the customer in a foreign system (Stripe, Aircall etc.) you're looking for.
ID of a view to filter customers by.
Primary email address of the customer you're looking for.
The customer's preferred language (format: ISO_639-1) to filter with.
Value indicating your position in the list of all customers. If omitted, the first customer of the list will be returned.

request.description: List customers, paginated, and ordered by their name (alphabetical order).

response.name: A list of customers.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the customer to delete.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

query.description: Maximum number of events to return.
Value indicating your position in the list of all events. If omitted, the first events of the list will be returned.
Attribute used to order events. Acceptable values: created_datetime, created_datetime:asc, created_datetime:desc
ID of the object associated with the events to return.
Type of the object associated with the events to return. Acceptable values: Account, Macro, Tag, Customer, Team, View, Widget, User, TicketMessage, Ticket, Rule, Integration
IDs of the users who triggered the events to return.
Types of the events to return.

request.description: List events, paginated, and ordered by their creation date, with the most recently created first.

response.name: A list of events.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

response.name: The created integration.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

query.description: Value indicating your position in the list of all integrations. If omitted, the first integration of the list will be returned.
Maximum number of integrations to return. Default: 100.
Type of integration.
Attribute used to order integrations. Acceptable values: created_datetime:asc, created_datetime:desc

request.description: List integrations matching the given parameters, paginated.

response.name: List of integrations according to the given criteria.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the integration to retrieve.

response.name: The requested integration.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the integration to update.

response.name: The updated integration.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the integration to delete.

request.description: Delete a integration. Any views that use this integration will be deactivated. Integrations currently used in rules and/or other integrations cannot be deleted.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

request.description: Create a job and schedule its execution. Jobs are processed asynchronously, meaning that once a job is created, it will run in background on Gorgias's servers. According to the type and size of the task, a job can take a few minutes or several hours to be executed. You can use our API to check the status of the job.

response.name: The created job.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

query.description: Filter jobs by status. Acceptable values: cancel_requested, canceled, done, errored, fatal_errored, pending, running, scheduled
Filter jobs by job type. Acceptable values: applyMacro, deleteTicket, updateTicket, exportTicket, exportMacro, importMacro, exportTicketDrilldown, exportConvertCampaignSalesDrilldown
The maximum number of objects to fetch per page. Default: 100.
Value indicating your position in the list of all jobs. If omitted, the first jobs of the list will be returned.
Attribute used to order jobs. Acceptable values: created_datetime:asc, created_datetime:desc

request.description: List jobs, paginated and ordered by their creation date, with the most recent ones first.

response.name: A list of jobs.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the job to retrieve.

response.name: The requested job.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the job to update.

response.name: The updated job.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the job to cancel.

request.description: Jobs can be canceled at any time but be aware that if a job already started, changes done by this one won't be reverted. For example, if you created a job to close 10k tickets and you cancel it at some point, these closed tickets won't be reopened.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

response.name: The created macro.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

query.description: Filter macros containing the given search query.
Filter macros containing any language in the list.
Filter macros containing all tags in the given list.
Number of relevant macros to return on top of the list.
The maximum number of macros to fetch per page. Default: 30.
The index of the page to fetch.

Order macros by the given attribute.
If `relevance` is specified, the `ticket_id` parameter is required, and macros will be ordered by the most relevant macros to reply to the given ticket. 
Acceptable values `name`, `created_datetime`, `update_datetime`, `usage`, `relevance` have been deprecated. Suffix values with `:asc` or `:desc`.

response.name: A list of macros.

request.description: Create a new satisfaction survey. If you don't want the satisfaction survey to be sent by Gorgias set `should_send_datetime` parameter to `null`.

request.description: List all views ordered by their `display_order` attribute, with the smallest position first. Current user variables (`{{current_user...}}`) present in views' filters will be replaced with the information of the user listing views.

query.description: Value indicating your position in the list of all the view's items. If omitted, the first items of the list will be returned.
ID of an item to ignore in the list of returned items.
Maximum number of items to return.

- `prev` returns items before the cursor.
- `next` returns items after the cursor.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the view to list items from.

request.description: List view's items, paginated, and ordered by the attribute specified in the data of view.

response.name: A list of view's items.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the ticket to delete.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

query.description:
Policy applied on external actions associated with the message if they failed.
Three actions are possible:
  - force: bypass the failed action and try to execute the remaining one and to send the message
  - retry: retry the failed action
  - cancel: delete the TicketMessage

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the ticket to create a message in.

request.description:
Create a message and either:
  - not send it if the attribute `sent_datetime` is filled.
  If you import a message that was already sent, make sure to set this value.
  - send it to the recipients if the attribute `sent_datetime` is empty.
  - add it to the ticket as an internal note if you specified this channel.
  Internal notes are not sent to your customers.

When a message is created, it does not mean that this one has been sent because the creation and sending process are decoupled. Messages are basically sent asynchronously. Once a message has been sent, Gorgias will automatically update the message to fill the time when it was sent (`sent_datetime`).

response.name: The created message.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the ticket to get the messages from.

request.description: List all messages of a ticket, ordered by their sending date, with the oldest messages first.

response.name: A list of messages.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: basic
